title: Tariff quotas are amended for agricultural goods in the FTA between Türkiye&Georgia
bodyHtml:
The Free Trade Agreement (FTA) signed between Turkey and Georgia on November 21, 2007, came into effect on November 1, 2008. With this FTA, customs duties, quantity restrictions, and equivalent taxes applied to all industrial products in imports were abolished between the parties, and some concessions were granted in certain products related to agricultural trade under Protocol I.

Changes have been made in some quota amounts and customs duty reductions for Georgia with Joint Committee Decision 1/2022, which regulates the agricultural concessions of the FTA's Protocol I. On the other hand, some items in which our country is a strong producer and has realized exports in high amounts have had their customs duties reduced to zero by Georgia. With these regulations, it is aimed to increase our exports to Georgia.

The Law stating that the decision amending Protocol I related to agricultural products has been approved was published today.

Click here for the approval law.

Click here to view tax concessions and designated quotas for agricultural products. (Taken from the Turkish Parliament website)

When importing into Georgia  the following products will benefit from a tariff reduction:

-Natural honey (GTIP code: 0409)

-Grapefruits and pomelos (GTIP code: 0805.40)

-Cigarettes containing tobacco (GTIP code: 2402.20)

Changes in importing to Türkiye;

Quotas are opened for;

0102.29 Livestock (excluding breeding animals)

0201, 0202 Fresh or chilled meat of bovine animals Frozen meat of bovine animals

0302.42, 0303.59.10 Fresh, chilled or frozen herring

0805.50 Lemons

0806.10 Grapes (fresh)

0810.50 Kiwis (fresh)

0813.20 Dried plums

0813.30 Dried apples

0813.40 Dried peaches, pears, papayas, guavas, and other edible fruits

0902.10 Green tea (unfermented) (in ready-made packages weighing not more than 3 kg) 

0902.20 Green tea (unfermented) (in ready-made packages weighing more than 3 kg)

1208.90 Flours and meals of oilseeds and oleaginous fruits (except soybean flour)

2002 Tomatoes (prepared or preserved otherwise than by vinegar or acetic acid)

2106.90 Food preparations not elsewhere specified or included in the tariff.

Quotas are increased for;

0409 Natural honey

2204 Fresh grape wine (including fortified wines); grape must (excluding those of heading 20.09)

Tariff quotas reduced for,

2007 (excluding 2007.99.97.00.14) Jams, jellies, marmalades, fruit or nut puree, and fruit or nut pastes (cooked preparations) whether or not containing added sugar or other sweetening matter.

2009 Fruit juices (including grape must) and vegetable juices, unfermented and not containing added alcohol, whether or not containing added sugar or other sweetening matter.
